semantic.role: Automates the build and deployment of project documentation to GitHub Pages on changes to documentation or package files.
responsibilities: Trigger documentation build and deploy on pushes to main that modify docs/, package.json, or package-lock.json
Build documentation using Node.js and npm
Deploy built documentation artifact to GitHub Pages
outOfScope: Deployment of other project artifacts (e.g., code, binaries)
Handling pull request previews or staging environments

English.purpose: This workflow ensures that whenever documentation or core project configuration files are updated on the main branch, the docs are rebuilt and published automatically to GitHub Pages. It reduces manual overhead for releasing documentation changes.
English.context_and_audience: Maintained by the ArchSpine core team. Relevant for any contributor who is responsible for keeping the project's documentation up to date and published. This workflow runs only on the main branch and only when specific paths are changed, so it is safe for day-to-day development.
key_takeaways: Triggers only on pushes to main that affect docs/, package.json, or package-lock.json
Builds documentation using npm ci and the docs:build script
Deploys the output (.vitepress/dist) to GitHub Pages using official actions
No testing or code deployment is included in this workflow
